points: Part IV (Articles 36-51): Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSPs), borrowed from Ireland.
Part IVA (Article 51A): Fundamental Duties, added by 42nd CAA (1976), borrowed from USSR.
Non-justiciable: Neither DPSPs nor Fundamental Duties can be directly enforced by writs under Art 32.
Dr. Ambedkar called DPSPs the 'Novel Features' of the Constitution.
Granville Austin called Part III & Part IV the 'Conscience of the Constitution'.
Article 39(d): Equal pay for equal work for men and women.
Article 39A: Equal justice and free legal aid (Added by 42nd CAA 1976).
Article 40: Organization of Village Panchayats (Gandhian Principle).
Article 44: Uniform Civil Code (UCC) for all citizens throughout India.
Article 45: Early childhood care & education for children below 6 years (Modified by 86th CAA).
Article 48A: Environment, forest & wildlife protection (Added by 42nd CAA 1976).
Article 50: Separation of Judiciary from Executive.
Article 51: Promotion of international peace and security (Basis of foreign policy).
Swaran Singh Committee: Recommended 8 duties, Parliament added 10 duties in 1976.
86th CAA (2002): Added 11th duty [Art 51A(k)] for parents to educate children aged 6-14.
Minerva Mills Case (1980): Balance between Fundamental Rights and DPSPs.
Duty to Pay Taxes & Duty to Vote are NOT listed under Article 51A.
